core_mechanism: Relaxation of an abruptly perturbed system possessing an extensive set of conserved quantities to a constrained maximum-entropy (generalized Gibbs) steady state rather than a single-parameter thermal equilibrium, exhibiting transient collapse-and-revival oscillations at a frequency set by the perturbation strength
brief_reason: The abstract reports that after a sudden change of the control parameter the system settles into a non-thermal steady state described by generalized Gibbs ensembles, with collapse-and-revival on a period inversely proportional to the interaction, which is exactly maximum-entropy relaxation under conserved constraints.
